name: Jito Block Engine
description: MEV-optimized transaction and bundle submission service using a modified Agave validator client (Jito-Solana). Bundles are executed sequentially and atomically (all-or-nothing). Tips are redistributed to validators and their stakers.

api_key.note: No approved auth key needed for default sends. Optional UUID auth available.

tip_wallet_note: Select randomly to reduce contention. Do NOT use Address Lookup Tables for tip accounts.
tip_note: 1000 lamports minimum for bundles. During high-demand periods, this minimum may not be sufficient. Use tip_floor endpoint for current competitive tips.
cost_on_failure: No cost if tip instruction is in the same transaction as the MEV logic. If the transaction reverts, the tip is not paid. WARNING: standalone tip transactions in bundles can still land if the bundle is on an uncled block (uncle bandit risk).

tip_strategy.sendTransaction: 70% priority fee / 30% Jito tip split recommended
tip_strategy.sendBundle: Only the Jito tip matters; priority fees are irrelevant for bundles

rate_limits.default: 1 request per second per IP per region
rate_limits.note: No auth key needed for default rate. Rate limits are enforced per-region, so using multiple regions gives higher aggregate throughput.

sendTransaction.description: Proxies to Solana sendTransaction but forwards directly to the Jito validator. Automatically sets skip_preflight=true. Default MEV protection enabled.

params.description: Base64 (recommended) or base58 (deprecated) encoded signed transaction

bundleOnly.description: When true, transaction is sent as a single-transaction bundle with revert protection (all-or-nothing). Cost-efficient for MEV protection.

sendBundle.description: Submits a bundle of up to 5 signed transactions for atomic sequential execution. All-or-nothing: if any transaction fails, the entire bundle is rejected.

params.description: Array of base64 or base58 encoded signed transactions

sendBundle.returns: Bundle ID (SHA-256 hash of transaction signatures)
sendBundle.requirements: Minimum tip of 1000 lamports required. Tip must be a SOL transfer to one of the 8 tip wallets.
sendBundle.auction: Bundles compete in parallel auctions at 50ms ticks. Priority based on tip/CUs-requested efficiency.

getBundleStatuses.description: Returns the status of submitted bundles. Searches recent slots plus up to MAX_RECENT_BLOCKHASHES (300) rooted slots.

params.description: Array of bundle IDs to query

returns.slot: u64 - slot where bundle was processed

getInflightBundleStatuses.description: Returns status of bundles submitted within the last 5 minutes.

params.description: Array of bundle IDs to query

getTipAccounts.description: Returns the 8 designated tip account addresses. Use to dynamically fetch tip wallets rather than hardcoding.

encoding.note: base64 is faster and recommended. base58 is slower and deprecated.

bundles.account_locking: Bundles with intersecting locking patterns [(w,w), (r,w), (w,r)] compete in the same auction. Non-intersecting bundles run in separate auctions.

revert_protection.description: bundleOnly=true on sendTransaction gives single-TX bundle revert protection. Full bundles are inherently revert-protected.
revert_protection.note: If a bundle transaction lands on an uncled block, it may be rebroadcast WITHOUT atomicity protection (uncle bandit risk).

sandwich_protection.description: Add any valid Solana pubkey starting with 'jitodontfront' to transaction instructions. Bundle is rejected unless your transaction appears at index 0 (first position).
sandwich_protection.account_requirement: Does not need to exist on-chain
sandwich_protection.compatibility: Works with sendBundle and sendTransaction. Supports AddressLookupTables.

mev_protection.description: Default MEV protection enabled on sendTransaction

shred_stream.description: Low-latency shred delivery service. Each regional endpoint has a shred receiver address (IP:port).

best_practices: Always include tip instruction in the same transaction as MEV logic to avoid paying on failure
Do NOT use Address Lookup Tables for tip accounts (reduces validator prioritization)
Select tip accounts randomly to reduce contention
Add post-transaction checks and account assertions for safety
Minimize standalone tip transactions in bundles (increases uncle bandit vulnerability)
Use base64 encoding (not base58) for faster processing
For sendTransaction: split 70% priority fee / 30% Jito tip
For sendBundle: only Jito tip matters, priority fees are irrelevant
